context: innomcp frontend debug wave. Evidence: browser VQA found frontend calling dead backend localhost:3011 (401 loop), relative fetches to nonexistent Next routes (404), test credentials exposed on login page. Docker now bakes NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:3015 at build. Backend REST lives at http://localhost:3015 (docker, maps to container :3011). Next.js app itself serves on :3000.

groups.mission: innomcp UI debug wave — central backend URL helper, fix broken fetches, hide test creds, VQA regression spec

members.task: You are a senior Next.js 14 (app router) TypeScript engineer. Write the COMPLETE content of a new file `src/app/lib/backendUrl.ts` for a Next.js frontend that talks to an Express backend. Requirements: (1) export const BACKEND: string — resolution order: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL if non-empty; else if running in browser (typeof window !== 'undefined') and window.location.port === '3000' use 'http://localhost:3015'; else '' (empty string means same-origin). (2) export const WS_BACKEND: string — process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL if non-empty; else derive from BACKEND by replacing leading http with ws; else ''. (3) export function backendFetch(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<Response> — prefixes path with BACKEND, always sets credentials: 'include', merges any provided init (init.headers must merge, not overwrite). (4) Strict TypeScript, no `any`, no external imports, works under `isolatedModules`. (5) Top-of-file comment (English, 2 lines max) explaining that scattered hardcoded localhost:3011 fallbacks are the bug this centralizes. Output ONLY the raw TypeScript file content. No markdown fences, no explanation.

You are a senior React/TypeScript engineer fixing a bug in `src/app/components/chat/ChatSidebar.tsx`. BUG: three fetch() calls use relative paths (/api/projects, /api/tasks) which hit the Next.js server on :3000 where those routes do not exist (404). They must call the Express backend instead via a central helper. The helper exists at `src/app/lib/backendUrl.ts` and exports `BACKEND: string` (e.g. 'http://localhost:3015' or '' for same-origin). Here are the three EXACT current code blocks:

BLOCK-A (inside createProject callback):
      const response = await fetch("/api/projects", {
        method: "POST",
        credentials: "include",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({ name: trimmed }),
      }).catch(() => null);

BLOCK-B (inside a useEffect loading projects):
    fetch("/api/projects", { credentials: "include" })
      .then((response) => (response.ok ? response.json() : null))

BLOCK-C (inside a useEffect loading recent tasks):
    fetch("/api/tasks?limit=8", { credentials: "include" })
      .then((r) => (r.ok ? r.json() : null))

Produce the corrected version of each block using template literal `${BACKEND}/api/...` (keep everything else byte-identical, same indentation), plus the single import line to add: import { BACKEND } from (correct relative path from src/app/components/chat/ to src/app/lib/). Output format EXACTLY:
IMPORT:
<import line>
BLOCK-A-NEW:
<corrected block A>
BLOCK-B-NEW:
<corrected block B>
BLOCK-C-NEW:
<corrected block C>
No markdown fences, no commentary.

You are a security-minded React engineer. In `src/app/login/page.tsx` a Test Credentials hint box (real admin/user/officer passwords) renders unconditionally — it must only render outside production. EXACT current block: {/* Test Credentials Hint */}

🔑 Test Credentials

Admin: admin@example.local / <REDACTED_PASSWORD>

User: user@example.local / <REDACTED_PASSWORD>

Officer: officer@example.local / <REDACTED_PASSWORD>

Produce the replacement block that wraps this EXACT JSX in {process.env.NODE_ENV !== "production" && ( ... )} preserving the comment line and all inner content byte-identical, with correct JSX syntax and indentation (keep 12-space base indent). Output ONLY the replacement block, no markdown fences, no commentary.

You are a QA automation engineer. Write the COMPLETE content of a Playwright spec file `tests/vqa-regression.spec.ts` using @playwright/test for a Next.js app at http://localhost:3000 (chat UI at /living-chat, login at /login). The spec must contain exactly these tests: (1) 'no requests to dead backend port 3011' — collect all request URLs during load of /living-chat (domcontentloaded + 5s settle), assert none contain 'localhost:3011'. (2) 'no 404 API responses' — collect responses during the same load, assert no response with status 404 whose URL contains '/api/'. (3) 'page has a title' — assert (await page.title()).length > 0. (4) 'no zero-size content panels' — evaluate elements matching 'main div' that have non-empty textContent, assert none with offsetWidth === 0 and offsetHeight === 0. (5) 'login page hides test credentials in production' — goto /login, read body text; this test must be skipped (test.skip) unless process.env.VQA_PROD === '1', and when run asserts body text does NOT contain 'Test Credentials'. Use a test.describe block 'VQA regression', test.setTimeout(30000) per test or describe-level, viewport 1440x900 via test.use. Strict TypeScript. Output ONLY the raw TypeScript file content, no markdown fences, no commentary.
